rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOBROCKE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 8, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment withdrawn. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to second cause of action (strict liability), third cause of action (false representation), fourth cause of action (loss of consortium), fifth cause of action (premises liability), and punitive damages request. Motion conceded as to false representation. No triable issue of fact regarding strict liability, no evidence defendant sold or supplied asbestos containing product that defendant was exposed to. No evidence of false representation to plaintiff, or that defendant controlled the premises or supervised plaintiff. Loss of consortium barred. (See Zwicker vs. Altamont Emergency Room Physicious Medical Group (2002) 98 Cal.App. 4th 26.) Objection to transcripts from prior cases overruled pursuant to Williams vs. Saga Enterprises, Inc. (1990) 225 Cal.App. 3d 142. However, testimony not sufficient to establish witnesses' relationship to defendant, and not sufficient to raise a triable issue of fact regarding punitive damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JOHNSON VS. AC AND S,INC.; et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 8, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion timely, court order did not bar this motion. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. See Weber vs. John Crane (2006) 143 Cal.App. 4th 1433; Gaggero vs. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App. 4th 884, 893. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JOHNSON VS. AC AND S,INC.; et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 8, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT INVENSYS SYSTEMS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion timely. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift burden of proof. The Court denies the motion because: (1) Most of the special discovery that defendant cites is unverified and as unverified responses is tantamount to nothing under Appleton vs. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App. 3rd 632, 636; (2) The discovery that is verified does not show that plaintiffs cannot establish their case; (3) The Hagberg deposition is inadmissible under Sprague vs. Equifax, Inc. (1985) 166 Cal.App. 3rd 1012, 1042; (4) Even if the court admitted the Hagberg deposition, he stated that he saw decedent working with defendant's recorders; (5) Hagberg's admission that he did not know whether decedent's work caused asbestos exposure is not probative because there is no showing that he has the requisite foundation regarding that issue; (6) Defendant's citation to the heirs' deposition testimony is not probative because there is no showing that they possess personal knowledge about decedents work environment; and (7) Defendant's separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350(h) because defendant generally cites evidence. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)
